Pitching the Deal
Clayton Makepeace
Module 	5

-- 1 of 17 --

Don‚Äôt Forget to Aim ...
3 Your 	goals:
¬Ą Fulfill client‚Äôs fondest business desires
¬Ą Eliminate his deepest frustrations
¬Ą Allay his fears
¬Ą Add something new
¬Ą Include all info an attorney would need
to begin drawing up your contract

-- 2 of 17 --

Win Before You Begin
3 Ask ...
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat is your mission statement?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat‚Äôs your short, medium and long‚Äźterm vision for your
company?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat challenges must be overcome to achieve your
vision?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat are your greatest personal frustrations in achieving
your vision?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat‚Äôs your greatest fear?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhy do you think people buy your product?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat are the most common causes for customer
complaints?‚ÄĚ

-- 3 of 17 --

Win Before You Begin
3 Ask 	...
¬Ą ‚ÄúWho are your strongest managers?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat are the weakest areas on your staff?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat have you tried that didn‚Äôt work ‚Äź‚Äź‚Äź and why
do you think it failed?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat do want from me?‚ÄĚ
¬Ą ‚ÄúWhat are you willing to pay me if I give you what
you want?‚ÄĚ

-- 4 of 17 --

The single most
important sales letter
you will ever write ...
3 Pointers ...
¬Ą Think of it as ‚ÄúB2B sales copy‚ÄĚ
¬Ą Respect your client‚Äôs time
¬Ą Respect your client‚Äôs intelligence
¬Ą Don‚Äôt forget: 	OPWR
¬Ą Anticipate and answer questions and objections
¬Ą Provide some value; not too much

-- 5 of 17 --

The Opportunity
3 Sell 	the 	dream
¬Ą Be specific
¬Ą Give it dimension
¬Ą Tell ‚Äúhow‚ÄĚ

-- 6 of 17 --

The Challenges
3 List the things that must be done to achieve the
vision
¬Ą Company, spokesperson and product positioning
¬Ą Product development
¬Ą Infrastructure
¬Ą Organization & Personnel
¬Ą Lead production and conversion
¬Ą New customer acquisition
¬Ą Customer service
¬Ą Retention
¬Ą Public Relations

-- 7 of 17 --

The Product
3 Outline 	your 	ideas for 	...
¬Ą Improving existing products
z Packaging
z Quality
z Price
z Guarantee
z Premiums
¬Ą Developing new products
¬Ą Acquiring third‚Äźparty products

-- 8 of 17 --

The Market
3 Where your ideal prospects are hiding ...
¬Ą Identify best customers‚Äô psychographics
¬Ą Identify the sources of best customers
¬Ą Show where similar prospects can be found

-- 9 of 17 --

The Media
3 Suggest new places to find your client‚Äôs ideal prospects ...
¬Ą The Web
z E‚Äźmail
z Affiliate campaigns
z E‚Äźzine sponsorships
z Banners
z Social networking sites
z Joint ventures
¬Ą Direct mail
¬Ą Print ads
¬Ą TV infomercials and/or spots
¬Ą Radio infomercials and/or spots
¬Ą Other

-- 10 of 17 --

The Strategy
3 Prioritize the promotions and projects that
you will undertake for the client ...
¬Ą List opportunities that will produce the greatest
benefit in the shortest time and at the lowest cost
first
¬Ą Include a rough timeline for each project
¬Ą 	Include a rough cost and target return for each

-- 11 of 17 --

The Relationship
3 Specify ...
¬Ą The work you will do for the client
z Marketing strategy
z Testing strategy
z Copywriting
z Graphics direction
z Product ideas/development
z General consulting: 	Brainstorming, consulting
on organization, recruitment, procedures,
resources, etc

-- 12 of 17 --

The Relationship
3 Specify ...
¬Ą How the work will be performed
z Touch‚Äźpoints
z Control
z Post‚Äźmortems
¬Ą What you will be paid for each type of work
z Advances
z Royalties
z Commissions
z Overrides
z Fees
z Retainers
z Stock options

-- 13 of 17 --

The Relationship
3 Specify ...
¬Ą When you will be paid
¬Ą How you can monitor your compensation
¬Ą Penalty for late payments
¬Ą That your relationship will be memorialized in
a legal agreement
¬Ą The term of the agreement
¬Ą The cancellation clause
¬Ą Use of your work product after cancellation

-- 14 of 17 --

The Next Step
3 Give your client a clear view of what
should happen next ...
¬Ą Meet to discuss your proposal, answer
questions, make adjustments
¬Ą Finalize the legal agreement
¬Ą Begin work

-- 15 of 17 --

Eight Fatal Errors
1. Lack of preparation
2. A reading assignment
3. Pompous language, business school jargon
4. Excess complexity; unnecessary detail
5. Failing to fully disclose your compensation
6. Blowing the deadline
7. Poor/delayed follow‚Äźup
8. Typos!

-- 16 of 17 --
